expectations.text: Places Stripe access in an adapter (src/adapters/) wired via Container, not in the service
expectations.evidence: 'goes in src/adapters/stripe/stripe-client.ts, not in modules/ ... The container is the only place new StripeClient(...) may appear.'

expectations.text: Places proration as a pure domain function (no I/O)
expectations.evidence: 'Proration is pure business computation ... vendor/shared/billing/proration.ts — zero framework imports ... no zod, no drizzle-orm, no fastify.'

expectations.text: Puts request-body validation as a Zod HTTP schema in routes.ts
expectations.evidence: 'a Zod schema at the top of routes.ts ... registered via the Fastify schema option ... Keep this to shape only.'

expectations.text: Persists invoices via a workspace-scoped repository mapping rows to a DTO
expectations.evidence: 'repository.ts is the only billing file that speaks Drizzle ... $inferSelect kept private behind toDomain()/toDb() ... Every query is workspace-scoped.'

expectations.text: Describes endpoints as thin handlers via getContext + one service call
expectations.evidence: 'Each handler does exactly three things: resolve context (getContext) -> call one service method -> send the reply.'

expectations.text: Puts shared Invoice/Plan types in vendor/shared/contracts with zero framework imports
expectations.evidence: 'vendor/shared/contracts/billing.ts ... never a raw Stripe object, never a Drizzle row. That shared contract is also what the Next.js client imports.'

expectations.text: States cross-module data access goes through services, not repositories
expectations.evidence: Not exercised by this prompt (single new module). The answer stresses the service as orchestration hub and inward dependency directions but does not state the cross-module-via-service rule explicitly.
